core_changes: Central runtime profiles and override precedence with a conservative 4 GiB fallback
Process-wide cancellation-aware Ollama coordinator and local API service
Staged managed-model lifecycle with normalized Modelfile hash metadata
End-to-end context accounting and request-specific compact tool schemas
Per-client session snapshots and owned generation lifecycle with terminal SSE states
Metadata-driven bounded tool runner with ordering, cancellation, timeouts, and deduplication
Fedora-first and Windows-native platform contracts for paths, processes, terminals, apps, and browsers
Fedora DBus Spotify backend preserved with explicit Windows capability behavior
Atomic critical persistence with malformed-data preservation
Coordinated embeddings, vision, vault, codebase, and routine execution
Electron exact-child ownership, readiness, singleton, and owner-token shutdown
Cross-platform PyInstaller and electron-builder configuration foundations
tests_run: earlier unittest discovery: 110 passed, 1 pre-existing pytest import error
core, CLI, web, and runtime imports passed
Electron main and preload syntax passed

phase2_tasks.task: Run native Windows 10 and 11 integration tests for runtime paths, terminals, shortcut-target-safe apps, browser/Spotify capability results, and exact process-tree termination.
phase2_tasks.acceptance_criteria: All supported native operations work without WSL or shell interpolation, shortcut targets are validated, and failures report the actual capability.

phase2_tasks.task: Build and smoke-test one Fedora AppImage and one Windows NSIS artifact.
phase2_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Bundled backend discovery, port readiness, singleton behavior, clean exact-owner shutdown, hidden Windows console, and user-data survival are verified.

phase2_tasks.task: Expand non-cooperative timeout, shutdown, nested execution, idempotency, exception-after-effect uncertainty, and side-effect ordering tests.
phase2_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Uncertain work cannot trigger later unsafe effects and shutdown remains bounded without deadlock.

phase2_tasks.task: Complete drive, mixed-separator, Unicode, reserved-name, locked-file, long-path, and supported UNC integration coverage.
phase2_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Every operation that claims Windows support has explicit passing path and failure tests.

phase2_tasks.task: Expand natural-language recall tests for request-specific tool-schema selection.
phase2_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Each registered model-exposed tool is selected for representative phrasing without exceeding low-VRAM first-turn context.

phase2_tasks.task: Resolve the pre-existing pytest collection dependency and run Fedora and Windows test matrices.
phase2_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Default full-suite discovery completes without collection errors on both platforms.

phase2_tasks.task: Audit packaged hidden imports, direct build/publish scripts, and optional native dependencies, then align artifact version naming.
phase2_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Clean-machine artifacts start and surface optional dependency failures without startup crashes.

phase2_tasks.task: Repair legacy /vault compatibility aliases that reference obsolete dispatch names.
phase2_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Every documented vault slash subcommand uses a registered tool path and has a bounded compatibility test.

phase2_tasks.task: Normalize malformed Ollama stream chunk failures into controlled CLI errors.
phase2_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Unexpected local API response shapes do not terminate the CLI process without a controlled diagnostic.

phase2_tasks.task: Complete documentation, migration notes, final support matrices, CI expansion, and repetitive per-tool cleanup.
phase2_tasks.acceptance_criteria: Phase 2 documentation and CI truthfully match verified Fedora and Windows behavior.

known_failures: Pre-existing tests/test_terminal_launcher.py collection error: pytest is not installed in the inspected environment.
nvidia-smi returned no usable VRAM data on the inspected host; automatic selection safely fell back to low-vram.
No live model generation, staged model rebuild, model download, personal data access, OAuth, AppImage build, or Windows NSIS build was performed in Phase 1.
